items.final_wording: Includes hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hypercholesterolaemia/hyperlipidaemia among the listed chronic conditions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases – 21
source.population: People receiving laboratory monitoring for listed chronic conditions, including hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hyperlipidaemia.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for listed chronic-condition laboratory monitoring in Dubai telehealth; partial for broad follow-up and arbitrary laboratory-result workflows.

exact_location.heading: 5.1 Chronic conditions that could be managed virtually

source.evidence_paraphrase: Includes hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hypercholesterolaemia/hyperlipidaemia among the listed chronic conditions.

items.rationale: Relevant only when a listed established thyroid condition is clinician entered.

items.final_wording: Specifies clinician review of TSH, free T4, and T3 over time and notes adherence or symptoms as reasons for earlier testing.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases – 21
source.population: People receiving laboratory monitoring for listed chronic conditions, including hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hyperlipidaemia.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for listed chronic-condition laboratory monitoring in Dubai telehealth; partial for broad follow-up and arbitrary laboratory-result workflows.

exact_location.heading: Appendix 2 — antithyroid and thyroxine therapy monitoring

source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies clinician review of TSH, free T4, and T3 over time and notes adherence or symptoms as reasons for earlier testing.

items.rationale: Relevant to recording already reviewed TSH, FT4 and T3 trends in treatment context without interpreting values or setting timing.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

source.title: Thyroid disease: assessment and management
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with suspected or diagnosed thyroid disease, subject to recommendation-specific age and disease qualifiers.
source.setting: Assessment, investigation, treatment discussion, monitoring, and follow-up of thyroid disease.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected thyroid-testing, thyroid-enlargement warning, hypothyroidism-monitoring, and hyperthyroidism-monitoring documentation. Symptom diagnosis, medicine selection or dose, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.2 — suspected dysfunction testing and antibodies

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

items.rationale: Relevant to clinician-entered indication, TSH, FT4, FT3 and biotin context without assigning thyroid status.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.

source.title: Thyroid disease: assessment and management
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with suspected or diagnosed thyroid disease, subject to recommendation-specific age and disease qualifiers.
source.setting: Assessment, investigation, treatment discussion, monitoring, and follow-up of thyroid disease.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected thyroid-testing, thyroid-enlargement warning, hypothyroidism-monitoring, and hyperthyroidism-monitoring documentation. Symptom diagnosis, medicine selection or dose, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.6 — follow-up and monitoring of primary hypothyroidism

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.

items.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-led monitoring of established primary hypothyroidism.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.

source.title: Thyroid disease: assessment and management
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with suspected or diagnosed thyroid disease, subject to recommendation-specific age and disease qualifiers.
source.setting: Assessment, investigation, treatment discussion, monitoring, and follow-up of thyroid disease.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected thyroid-testing, thyroid-enlargement warning, hypothyroidism-monitoring, and hyperthyroidism-monitoring documentation. Symptom diagnosis, medicine selection or dose, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.5 — follow-up and monitoring of hyperthyroidism

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.

items.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-led monitoring of established hyperthyroidism.
